title: Mildly Winey Japanese Peach Mochi
description: A low-sodium, wine-infused Japanese dessert featuring peach juice and a pasta-water-emulsion, formed into a delicate mochi, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the Japanese rice flour and granulated sugar.
Gradually add in the peach juice, white wine, and pasta water, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a small amount of unsalted butter in the center of each.
Fold the dough over the butter to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Repeat with the remaining dough and butter.
Serve the mochi warm, or let cool and store in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
